page_geometry.verdict: identical page size. The one label whose move would have narrowed the page (grounded source return, the rightmost element) was left open, so no absolute-pixel coordinate artifact appears.

genuinely_new_in_C3.cause: the 'driven electrode' label moved 0.25 cm toward its own electrode-bar referent, so 'driven' now sits nearer the bar; sub-threshold, untiered, leader still connects, after-PNG reads clean.

metric_shifted_preexisting.note: the 'cantilever' text_on_path already existed in C2; the 0.145 cm move of the floating-polymer-cantilever label shifted its metric DOWN (0.14 -> 0.112), i.e. slightly improved. Not a new finding.

visual_clash.verdict: one genuinely new sub-threshold near_miss ('driven', 0.019, tier null); no new tiered/promoted finding; one pre-existing text_on_path metric improved.

panel_boundary_coverage.verdict: crossings 6 -> 4: both repaired labels' crossings removed; the 4 remaining belong to the 2 labels deliberately left open (grounded substrate, grounded source return).

regression_verdict: PASS (machine) with one disclosed caveat: 2 of 4 accidental frame-crossing labels repaired (crossings 6 -> 4), no new tiered/promoted finding, page geometry unchanged, undeclared_geometry still 0. Caveat: one sub-threshold untiered near_miss on 'driven' (dark 0.019) from moving that label toward its referent; it is not a real collision (leader connects, PNG clean). Strict compile still blocked by the pre-existing check_visual_clash failure (identical mechanism as C1/C2), unrelated to this repair.
open_items: grounded substrate (Panel E): boxed between the left frame and the substrate rectangle; no position/anchor-only fit without collision or detachment. Human decision needed (widen panel, shorten text, or accept overhang).
grounded source return (Panel F): must overlap the HV wiring / motif / its own referent to clear the right frame. Human decision needed.
